one_time_description: Loss on transfer of water and wastewater assets to Atlantic First Nations Water Authority (AFNWA) effective April 1, 2023
economic_notes: First Nation operates tobacco fund, commercial fisheries, gas bar, VLT gaming, Bayside Development Corporation, and Bayside Operations. Entered agreement to purchase new vessel and vessel upgrades for $914,413 funded 96% by Atlantic Integrated Commercial Fisheries Initiative. Partnership investments in E'sukutimkewey Limited Partnership and Mi'kmaq Resource Development Partnership with 12 other Nova Scotia First Nations. Transferred water and wastewater assets to AFNWA.
notes: Net financial liabilities position (net debt) of $2,753,379. Large promissory note receivable of $48,464,229 from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership. Significant deferred revenue of $13,790,569 primarily from ISC ($12,619,524). New FNFA loan of $14,991,014 at 4.28% taken out during the year increasing long-term debt substantially from $49.9M to $63.6M. Loss on transfer of water assets to AFNWA ($2,148,059) is a non-recurring item. Government transfers detail not fully broken out in available text; ISC receivable used as proxy for ISC funding. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. Segmented schedule referenced but detailed figures not fully extracted from available pages.
